narrative: In FY2023-24, Tata Steel remains the strongest employee-diversity performer, leading female employee share at 8.36% and female wage share at 7.21%. Jindal Stainless leads Board gender diversity at 25.00% female directors, while Tata and JSW are tied at 20.00%; SAIL trails at 12.50%. SAIL dominates wellbeing expenditure at 1.46% of revenue, far ahead of Jindal at 0.30%, Tata at 0.12% and JSW at 0.035%, although Jindal’s later FY2024-25 BRSR indicates its FY2023-24 wellbeing number should have been 0.03%, not 0.30%. On KMP gender diversity, the entire peer set drops to 0% female KMPs, erasing SAIL’s FY2022-23 lead. Overall, Tata is strongest on workforce-level representation and wage share, Jindal is strongest at Board level, and SAIL is strongest on welfare-spend intensity. The laggard depends on metric: Jindal for employee and wage representation, JSW for wellbeing spend, and SAIL for Board percentage.
